title: Grassy German Steak Skewers
description: A light and refreshing German dish featuring fillet of steak, double-fry style, with a pescatarian-friendly twist, incorporating grassy and springy flavors.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together lemon juice, olive oil, parsley, dill, garlic powder, salt, and pepper.
Add the steak cubes to the bowl and marinate for at least 30 minutes.
In a separate bowl, mix together potato starch and a pinch of salt.
Remove the steak from the marinade and coat in the potato starch mixture, shaking off excess.
Dip the coated steak cubes in buttermilk, making sure they are fully submerged.
Dredge the steak cubes in the potato starch mixture again, pressing the coating onto the meat.
Thread the coated steak cubes onto skewers, leaving a small space between each piece.
Place the skewers on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper and bake for 20 minutes, flipping halfway through.
Increase the oven temperature to broil and cook for an additional 2-3 minutes, or until golden brown.
